romanized: Gān jú ào lì ào mài xuán fēng

food_subgroup: Soft serve ice cream dessert with cookie mix-in

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general ice cream food product class; no specific McFlurry or soft serve with cookie mix-in class exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Ice cream and similar frozen desserts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW covers ice cream and similar frozen desserts; no specific soft serve with cookie mix-in subcategory exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for McFlurry-type soft serve dessert identified in published SNOMED CT hierarchy; null used to avoid fabrication.

main_ingredients: soft serve ice cream (milk, sugar, cream, skimmed milk powder, emulsifiers)
OREO cookie pieces (wheat flour, sugar, cocoa, palm oil, emulsifiers)

serving.natural_language_portion: One regular McDonald's McFlurry cup weighing approximately 220 g, as served at McDonald's Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's USA Nutrition Information — McFlurry with OREO Cookies (medium); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for vanilla soft serve ice cream and OREO cookie crumbles for mandarin orange flavour adaptation
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Corporation Nutrition Centre / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore does not publish full per-100g nutrient breakdowns publicly. Values are adapted from McDonald's USA published nutrition data for the OREO McFlurry (medium, ~337 g) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for vanilla soft serve (FDC ID 167579) and chocolate sandwich cookies (FDC ID 174938). Mandarin orange flavouring contributes negligible macronutrient change relative to vanilla base; values adjusted proportionally to per-100g basis. This is an estimated adaptation; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — ice cream and dairy desserts; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Mandarin Orange OREO McFlurry. GI of 62 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables for ice cream with cookie/biscuit mix-ins (GI range 55–68 reported for similar dairy-based frozen desserts with added sugar and cookie pieces). A midpoint value of 62 is used as the best defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 62 × 64.9 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 40.2 g GL.

hypertension.guidance: Each regular serving provides approximately 231 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption and account for this within their daily sodium target of under 2000 mg, particularly if consuming other processed foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 48 g of sugar and a glycemic load of approximately 40 per serving, this dessert causes a significant postprandial blood glucose rise and should be avoided or consumed only as an occasional treat with careful carbohydrate budgeting by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: One regular serving contributes approximately 7 g of saturated fat and 48 g of added sugar, both of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dessert to occasional indulgence within an overall heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mandarin Orange OREO McFlurry is a limited-edition soft serve ice cream dessert sold at McDonald's Singapore outlets. It consists of McDonald's signature vanilla-based soft serve ice cream infused with mandarin orange flavouring, blended and swirled with crushed OREO chocolate sandwich cookie pieces. The dessert is machine-dispensed into a clear plastic cup and served cold. The flavour profile combines the light, citrusy sweetness of mandarin orange with the rich, chocolatey crunch of OREO cookie crumbles, creating a contrast of smooth and crunchy textures. It is classified as an ultra-processed food due to the use of emulsifiers, artificial flavourings, and highly refined ingredients. Nutritionally, a regular serving (approximately 220 g) provides around 392 kcal, 29.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g (predominantly sugar at 22 g per 100 g), 5.5 g fat, and 3.6 g protein per 100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 62 (medium), making it a high-glycemic-load food per serving. It is halal-certified and vegetarian but contains milk, wheat, gluten, and soy allergens. It is not suitable for individuals with lactose intolerance, coeliac disease, or those managing Type 2 Diabetes without careful carbohydrate planning. The product is a seasonal or promotional item and may not be available year-round. It is priced at SGD 4.20 at participating McDonald's Singapore outlets as listed on field survey data.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore does not publish full per-100g nutrient data publicly. Nutrient values are adapted from McDonald's USA published nutrition information for the OREO McFlurry and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for vanilla soft serve and OREO cookie crumbles. The mandarin orange flavouring is assumed to contribute negligible macronutrient change relative to the vanilla base. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Confidence is medium due to the adapted and estimated nature of the nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on McDonald's USA OREO McFlurry nutrition data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries; McDonald's Singapore does not publish full per-100g nutrient breakdowns. 2. Mandarin orange flavouring is assumed to contribute negligible macronutrient change relative to the vanilla soft serve base. 3. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables for ice cream and dairy desserts with cookie mix-ins; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. 4. Serving size of 220 g is estimated based on McDonald's Singapore regular McFlurry cup size; exact weight may vary. 5. Vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the adapted McDonald's USA nutrition panel or the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this adaptation. 6. The product is a limited-edition or seasonal item at McDonald's Singapore and may not be permanently available. 7. Halal status is based on McDonald's Singapore halal certification as listed in field survey data cuisine tags. 8. The food_id GD000125 is a field survey data seed record and is not present in the FD series food index.
